title: Optimize the Worst Piece
subtitle: Improve before you invent
summary: When there is no tactic, find the piece with the fewest useful futures and improve it.
attribution: Original prose; a common modern coaching habit.

steps.title: No tactic? Improve something
steps.markdown: If there is no forcing move, ask which piece is doing the least. In this position, the knight on b1 is still asleep while the rest of the position is developing.

steps.title: Improve the quiet piece
steps.markdown: Find the calm developing move that brings the least active white piece into the game.

hints.text: Look at the piece that has not moved yet.
The b1 knight can join the center.

feedback.correct: Yes. Nc3 improves the sleeping knight and adds more control over the center.
feedback.wrong: Look for the undeveloped piece with the least influence.

steps.title: The planning question
steps.markdown: When no check, capture, or threat works, what question keeps your move useful?

options.label: Which piece has the worst future?
options.explain: Right. Improving the worst piece raises the level of the whole position.

options.label: Can I move the same piece again?
options.explain: Sometimes, but repeated moves need a reason. First find the piece that is least active.

options.label: Can I make a random pawn move?
options.explain: Pawn moves are permanent. A quiet piece improvement is often safer and more flexible.

steps.title: Small improvements compound
steps.markdown: Strong players do not need every move to be dramatic. When tactics are absent, improve the piece with the fewest useful options.
